prerequisites: A ServiceNow instance and its base URL (for example https://your-instance.service-now.com), which is the host the REST Table API is called against at /api/now/table/{tableName}.
A ServiceNow user account (a sys_user record) whose username and password are used for HTTP Basic authentication on inbound REST requests.

That user must have read access to the tables you want to extract; ServiceNow evaluates table ACLs against the authenticated user's roles, so grant a role that permits REST read access (for example the rest_service role plus the role required by the target table, such as itil for ITSM tables like incident).

authMethods.name: Basic authentication (username and password)
authMethods.summary: ServiceNow inbound REST APIs support HTTP Basic authentication using the username and password of a ServiceNow sys_user record. This connector maps to that method: supply the account's username (config field username) and its password (secret field password). On each request the credentials are sent as a Basic auth header, and ServiceNow evaluates the user's roles against the table ACLs to authorize access.

setupSteps.title: Identify your instance base URL
setupSteps.body: Determine the URL of your ServiceNow instance, typically https://your-instance.service-now.com. The Table API is reached at this host under /api/now/table/{tableName}. Enter this value in the base_url config field.

setupSteps.title: Prepare a user account with the right access
setupSteps.body: Use an existing ServiceNow user (sys_user record) or create one for integration use. Ensure the account can read the tables you plan to extract: ServiceNow checks table ACLs against the user's roles, so assign a role that grants REST read access (for example rest_service together with the table's required role, such as itil for ITSM tables like incident).

setupSteps.title: Provide Basic authentication credentials
setupSteps.body: Enter the account's username in the username config field and its password in the password secret field. The connector sends these as HTTP Basic authentication on each inbound REST request to the instance.

setupSteps.title: Verify connectivity
setupSteps.body: Confirm the credentials work by querying the Table API, for example GET {base_url}/api/now/table/{tableName} (such as the incident table) with Basic auth. A successful authenticated response confirms the base_url, username, and password are correct and the user has read access.

sources.title: REST API authentication requirements for inbound requests (ServiceNow Now Support KB0793963)
